mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead. Ideal for teams looking to streamline their AI agent operations in a production environment.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kagenti cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kagenti cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

kagenti create cluster --name kagenti-cluster --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to interact with your new Kagenti cluster.

```bash

kagenti kubeconfig --cluster kagenti-cluster --output ~/.kube/config

```

> This command generates a kubeconfig file for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kagenti cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step enables autoscaling and sets up monitoring and RBAC for your Kagenti cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.4.0-alpha.1/deploy/rbac.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for the cluster.
> This command configures RBAC for security.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Kagenti cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

kagenti delete cluster --name kagenti-cluster

rm -f ~/.kube/config

```

> This command deletes the cluster and all its resources.
> This command removes the kubeconfig file associated with the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kagenti CLI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step upgrades the Kagenti CLI to the latest version.

```bash

kagenti backup --cluster kagenti-cluster

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command backs up your current cluster configurations.
> This command downloads and installs the latest CLI version.
> Verify that all nodes are still in a 'Ready' state after the upgrade.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified region may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in a different region or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig file not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file was not generated or is missing.

**Fix:**
Run the kubeconfig setup command again to regenerate the file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node status: NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node may not have joined the cluster properly.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs for errors and ensure it meets the resource requirements.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with support for cluster creation, autoscaling, and monitoring.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
